what_it_does: Records a metadata attachment that links a design-source channel memory to an image resource at image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-10.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to deck1-2026/p-10
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
File lives in the channel_attachments directory
standouts: Target image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-10 references a specific deck slide
concerns: No caption, description, or reason for the attachment is provided
The target URI cannot be resolved or verified from this file alone
No author or provenance field is recorded
notes: A metadata stub that attaches an image (deck1-2026/p-10) to a design-source channel memory.
questions: What does the image deck1-2026/p-10 depict?
Which specific channel memory entry does this attachment belong to?
Why was this image attached to the design-source channel?
Who added the attachment and under what context?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it records an image attachment for a channel memory.
intention_signals: To create a traceable, timestamped link between a design-source channel memory and a specific image asset in a deck.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI target rather than a filesystem path.
contribution: Provides a persistent, timestamped attachment record that associates a design-source channel with an image asset, enabling retrieval of visual context for that channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file declaring an images attachment targeting image://design-source/deck1-2026/p-10 for the design-source channel, added on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To record that a specific image asset is attached to a design-source channel memory for later reference.
